content: The FDA has multiple approval pathways with different review timelines and evidence requirements. Standard review applies to drugs offering modest improvements over existing treatments, with a 10-month review clock from NDA submission. Priority review (6-month clock) is granted for drugs treating serious conditions with potential significant improvements in safety or efficacy. Breakthrough Therapy designation (for serious/life-threatening conditions) provides intensive FDA guidance, organizational commitment, and eligibility for accelerated approval if preliminary clinical evidence shows substantial improvement over available therapies.
